steps.text: The equation $14x + 15y = 436$ has the form (price)(quantity) + (price)(quantity) = total revenue. The numbers 14 and 15 are dollar prices per shirt; $x$ and $y$ are quantities sold.
Since $x$ and $y$ are quantities, the two choices saying $y$ is a price (C and D) are immediately eliminated.
The coefficient of $y$ is 15, the higher price. Long-sleeved shirts cost more than short-sleeved shirts, so $y$ = number of long-sleeved shirts sold and $x$ = number of short-sleeved shirts sold.

• A — Number of long-sleeved shirts sold: paired with the 15-dollar price, consistent with long-sleeved costing more. That confirms A is correct.
  • B — Number of short-sleeved shirts sold: short-sleeved would be the cheaper item, corresponding to the 14-dollar coefficient (the $x$ term), not $y$. Eliminated.
  • C — Price of each long-sleeved shirt: the variables are quantities, not prices. Eliminated.
  • D — Price of each short-sleeved shirt: same reason — variables are quantities. Eliminated.
